refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) for operational use, ensuring data accuracy and availability for all relevant stakeholders.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly address timeliness, data accuracy, and availability, which are critical for operational effectiveness and safety in a high-risk environment.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of worksite model updates under representative operational scenarios; verify data accuracy through cross-validation with sensor inputs; confirm availability via system uptime and interface responsiveness tests.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within 1.0 s latency.
FR2: Update the centralized worksite model with processed data ensuring consistency and accuracy.
FR3: Distribute updated worksite model data to all relevant modules and stakeholders within 1.0 s latency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Real-time telemetry ingestion and preprocessing pipeline.
DP2: Centralized worksite model database with consistency management.
DP3: Low-latency data distribution service using optimized network protocols.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address sub-FRs for timely acquisition, update, and distribution of worksite data.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between telemetry ingestion and model update due to latency constraints.
Dependency of distribution service on both ingestion and model update.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DPs are logically coupled but designed with clear interfaces to minimize diffusion and maintain modularity.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link supports required bandwidth and latency.
Risk of data inconsistency if ingestion or update delays occur.
High safety criticality demands robust error handling and fallback mechanisms.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicit error detection and recovery DPs to mitigate risks.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system integration and coordination of telemetry ingestion, worksite model update, and data distribution.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data streams from Vehicle and other sensors.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data communication between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model data and commands to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Communicates safety-related updates to Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes worksite model update logic and data distribution, minimizing diffusion.
Primarily data source and actuator; limited containment of update logic.
Consumes updated data but does not perform update logic.
Receives alerts but does not participate in update processing.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing the worksite model update and distribution within the NGHE Solution module reduces diffusion and improves maintainability and safety compliance.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck in NGHE Solution if update processing is not optimized.
High complexity within a single module may increase verification effort.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate partitioning within NGHE Solution if complexity or performance bottlenecks arise.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DPs logically satisfy the refined FRs with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Centralization in NGHE Solution may lead to module complexity.
Interfaces are well-defined minimizing unintended diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support the chosen IM boundary; interfaces align with known system boundaries.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via latency measurements and interface testing at the NGHE Solution boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibilities to NGHE Solution with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor NGHE Solution complexity and consider sub-module decomposition if needed.
Add explicit error handling and fallback mechanisms in DP and IM layers.

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment meets the requirements and constraints with acceptable risk and verification feasibility.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within 1.0 s latency.
FR2: Update the centralized worksite model with processed data ensuring consistency and accuracy.
FR3: Distribute updated worksite model data to all relevant modules and stakeholders within 1.0 s latency.

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network link supports required bandwidth and latency.
Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
Safety criticality demands robust error handling.
open_risks: Potential performance bottleneck in NGHE Solution module.
Data inconsistency risk if ingestion or update delays occur.
evidence_needed: Verification of data accuracy and consistency.
Performance profiling of NGHE Solution module.
